name: Hilton Honors American Express Aspire Card
effective_cost: Low if you use the resort + flight credits

verdict.text: Keep if you actually book Hilton resort stays — the credits are narrow

earn_rates.category: Flights (direct or Amex Travel), select car rentals, U.S. restaurants

earn_note: 14× only applies to Hilton-direct hotel bookings — third-party OTAs earn nothing. The 7× tier is broad enough (flights, some car rentals, US dining) to matter for everyday spend.

points.note: Hilton Honors points are issued generously (highest point-earning rates of any hotel program) but redeem for roughly half the value of Marriott Bonvoy or bank currencies. Never transfer to airlines — the ~10:1 ratio guts the value.

transfer_partners.highlight: 26 airline transfer partners, but almost all at a poor 10:1 ratio (a few exceptions like Virgin Atlantic at 20:3) — Hilton points are built to be spent on hotel stays, not transferred out.
transfer_partners.recent_changes: No confirmed recent changes to the airline transfer chart itself.

credits.name: Flight Credit
credits.subtitle: $50/quarter
credits.description: Up to $50 in statement credits each quarter on flights booked directly with an airline, AmexTravel.com, or the Amex Travel app.
tips: Quarterly with no rollover — four separate windows to hit, not one lump sum.

credits.name: CLEAR Plus
credits.subtitle: airport security
credits.description: Statement credit covering a CLEAR+ membership after you pay for it.
tips: $0 to you if you don't fly through CLEAR airports.

credits.name: Hilton Resort Credit
credits.subtitle: $200 × 2 · semi-annual
credits.description: Up to $200 in statement credits twice a year on eligible purchases made directly with participating Hilton Resorts.
tips: Only applies at a specific list of participating resort properties, not any Hilton — check the list before assuming a stay qualifies.
No rollover between halves — an unused H1 doesn't carry into H2.

credits.name: Annual Free Night Reward
credits.subtitle: no point cap · after card renewal
credits.description: One free night reward from Hilton Honors in your first year of card membership and every year on renewal, with no stated point-value ceiling.
tips: Unlike Marriott's capped free night, this one has no published point ceiling — but real-world value still depends entirely on which property you redeem it at.
$250 here is a conservative estimate, not a guarantee for any specific hotel.

insurance.detail: Primary outside the US up to $75,000; secondary worldwide, 30 days (excl. Australia, Italy, New Zealand)

protection_note: Solid but not top-tier — trip cancellation and rental coverage are comparable to other premium Amex cards, but this card no longer carries airport lounge access, which used to be part of its pitch.
rental_note: Rental coverage is primary outside the US but only secondary domestically — put US rentals on a card with primary US coverage if you rent often.

status_perks.name: Hilton Honors Diamond Status
status_perks.note: Top Hilton tier, complimentary with the card — 100% bonus points on stays, space-available room upgrades, premium Wi-Fi, and executive lounge access at select properties.

additional_cards.title: Additional Cards

benefits.text: No annual fee for authorized users
Earns Hilton Honors points on their spend (into your account)
Does not get their own Free Night Reward or Diamond status independently

additional_cards.note: Authorized users are free — an easy way to pool household spend toward the 14× Hilton rate.

timeline.text: Complimentary Priority Pass Select lounge access fully phased out for all cohorts of cardholders (the last group's benefit ended on this date).
Annual fee raised from $450 to $550, alongside a restructured benefits set: new quarterly flight credit and semi-annual resort credit replaced the prior benefit lineup.
